lever_summary: Wire the pass67 dense i64 storage into the i64 reduction paths. The full reduction (eval_reduce integral branch) folds LiteralBuffer::as_i64_slice() directly; the axis reduction (eval_reduce_axes integral branch) drives the OutIndexOdometer over the contiguous i64 slice. Both skip the per-element Literal::I64 match and the 24-byte enum stride.

alien_graveyard.primitive: dense SoA / typed columnar storage applied to reductions
alien_graveyard.mapping: consume the contiguous i64 backing store added in mcqr.30.13 so integer reductions become tight native-i64 folds the compiler can vectorize, instead of tagged-union materialization + match

method: Same-worker comparison on ts2: dense (new_i64_values / vector_i64) input vs a Vec<Literal>-backed reference bench run in the same process, isolating the data-model win from cross-worker CPU variance.

full_reduce_64k_i64.note: headline: flat fold, no odometer overhead, so the data-model win dominates

axis1_inner_contiguous_i64.note: secondary: the pass66 odometer already removed the dominant per-element decode, so the residual data-model win here is ~2x (no-gaps completeness)

command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'reduce_sum_64k_i64|reduce_sum_256_axis._i64' --save-baseline {i64full|i64red}

score.basis: headline full-reduce 16.68x conservative; axis reductions ~2x are no-gaps completeness on the same lever

change.lever: eval_reduce integral branch: as_i64_slice() fast fold returning Value::scalar_i64; eval_reduce_axes integral branch: as_i64_slice() drives the odometer. Both fall back to the unchanged Vec<Literal> loop when as_i64_slice() is None.

isomorphism.ordering: unchanged: the i64 slice is folded in ascending flat order (full) / odometer order (axis), identical to the generic loops
isomorphism.integer_op: unchanged: same int_init seed and int_op (ReduceSum a+b, ReduceProd a*b, ReduceMax i64::max, ReduceMin i64::min) applied in the same order; overflow behavior identical (same op) on both paths
isomorphism.index_mapping: axis path reuses OutIndexOdometer.next_index(), unchanged from pass66
isomorphism.errors: as_i64_slice() is Some only for I64 dense storage (element count matches shape); I32 / Literal-backed / malformed declared-I64 inputs return None and run the unchanged generic loop (which still type-checks each element)
isomorphism.output_dtype: unchanged: full -> scalar_i64; axis -> DType::I64 tensor

validation.name: dense_i64_reduce_parity
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax --lib reduction
validation.result: passed: 31 tests incl. dense_i64_reduce_bit_identical_to_literal_path (Sum/Prod/Max/Min, full + all axis subsets, negatives + zero)

validation.name: clippy_deny_warnings
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-lax --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings
